visa_types.name: Sports Purpose
visa_types.documents_required:
Travel insurance is mandatory for all Schengen countries. If you have not purchased, please  click here (https://visa.vfsglobal.com/are/en/deu/travel-insurance)
Kindly refer to the list of requisite documents below before submitting your application; this will minimize chances of rejection of your Visa Application due to incorrect or inadequate
                                  documents
The original passport must be submitted along with the application on the day of the appointment. Cabin crew/Pilots will be allowed to withdraw their passports  from the respective application centre in Dubai or Abu Dhabi within 1 to 3 working days after their application is submitted at the Consulate.
Passports MUST be resubmitted at the Visa Application Centre within 10 to 12 calendar days from the date at which it was withdrawn. In case an applicant is aware in advance that this will not be possible due to professional reasons, the VFS counter staff must be informed to make a note on their application/checklist.
Instructions for  applicants below 18 years
- For all children below 18 years, both parents need to be present at the Visa Application Centre to sign the VIDEX form.  If you are applying for a visa for a child under 12 years old, they must also attend an appointment, but don’t have to provide their fingerprint information.
- In case one parent is out of the country during the application process, the signed authorization form and passport copy with exit stamps from UAE must be provided to complete the
                                    process.
- All applicants below 18 years must submit a signed letter of consent or authorization form and passport copy of the parent.
Click here to download the authorisation form sample.
Please ensure that you are well informed on the New Entry Regulations (https://uae.diplo.de/ae-en/service/05-VisaEinreise/-/2466976) for Germany.

visa_types.overview:
Overview
Applicant will have to provide evidence that he/she falls under this category, kindly refer to the information sheet of documents required
PLEASE NOTE:
- Effective 11 June 2017, citizens of Ukraine can travel to Schengen countries without a prior visa for a period of up to 90 days. Please note that this exemption is only for holders of a Ukraine biometric passport. This does not apply to Ukrainian Nationals who are not holders of biometric passports and travelling for work during the stay period of 90 days.
- Effective 28 March 2017, citizens of Georgia can travel to Schengen countries without a prior visa for a period of up to 90 days. Please note that this exemption is only for holders of a Georgian biometric passport.
- Effective 15 March 2016, Peruvian nationals do not require a Schengen visa to travel to Germany for a period of 90 days or less within a timeframe of 190 days.

Declaration of Consentc

## Photo Specifications
Two recent biometric passport sized photographs (3.5x4.5 cm) as per specifications.
 Click here to view a sample biometric photo

Requirements:
- NEW! Should NOT be older than 6 months
- A frontal view of the face covering 67.20 -80% (32-26mm) of the photo
- A uniform and white-colored background
- Clear facial features and eyes – not covered by hair or glasses frame
- Head coverings are only accepted for religious reasons
- Do not glue or staple the pictures to the application form

## Processing Time
The processing time for the application at the German Consulate takes up to 15 calendar days.
             
 Please be advised that due to the above mentioned minimum processing time, we are not able to guarantee the return of passports by specific dates for travel. 
                                  
Please be sure to submit your visa application well in advance, to avoid any inconveniences.

## Download Form
Below forms are required to be completed by each applicant to submit the visa application:
- Schengen Visa Application Form (https://videx.diplo.de/videx/visum-erfassung/videx-kurzfristiger-aufenthalt) (To be filled online - mandatory)
- Insurance Declaration Form (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/5BpmWlHkYKfUfWB3GuF3Du/7e038c5b6b95e25003740f7e2290d4e8/insurance-declaration-form.pdf)
- Declaration of Consent (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/2qM3R74t9pbZeNWq1cWMZO/1979b62ad5fa190c772d5480f7bfca59/declaration-of-consent.pdf)
All applicants below 18 years, must submit a signed letter of consent or authorization form and passport copy of the parents. Click here to download the authorisation form sample.
The Schengen Visa Application form must be filled either in English/German. 
Instructions to complete an online form: 
- Click on the form link above
- When the page opens complete the form
- For clarifications refer to the guidance (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/3jaqGLq16RSzLhEMevzkUE/5cbd5c49bdd79e0637dd1ae1337bae9e/VIDEX-General-User-Guidelines.pdf) instructions.
- Once complete print out the form on clear paper
- The complete the printed set of your application form including the last barcoded page of your application form should be presented while submitting your application.
- The barcode page contains the necessary data in condensed and machine readable format.

- The barcode page contains the necessary data in condensed and machine readable format.
- If you are not able to fill the form online, a form filling service is available at the Visa Application Centre.

visa_types.name: Schengen Visa, Category C
visa_types.overview:
Schengen Rule
Schengen visa has to be obtained from the Embassy/Consulate of the country where the main purpose of travel lies. Therefore, please check your travel plans before making your application to the Consulate
                            of Germany. In case of travel to multiple Schengen countries, please make your application to the Embassy/Consulate where you will be staying for the maximum number of days. If the duration of
                            stay is the same in more than one Schengen country, please submit your application from the point of first entry. 
Do I need a Visa for Germany?
Click here (https://www.auswaertiges-amt.de/en/visa-service/visa-navigator/2489244) to check if you require a visa to enter Germany. 
Schengen Visa and Visa Code
In order to apply for a Schengen visa, the applicant needs to provide the documents as per the purpose of the trip. In individual cases, additional documents may be requested. Presentation of the
                            documents does not automatically lead to the issuance of a visa and possession of a visa does not confer an automatic right of entry. There is no automatic entitlement or legal right to a Schengen
                            visa. The ultimate decision regarding admission to the Schengen territory lies with the border authorities upon arrival.
The Visa Code standardizes the visa requirements which must be examined by the mission in the course of the visa procedure. The respective mission makes a decision on the visa application at its
                            own discretion, taking into account all the circumstances in any given individual case.
All visa applicants for Germany are advised to schedule an appointment and visit the Joint Visa Application Centre in Dubai to submit their applications, together with all necessary documents
Processing time for a visa may take up to 15 days. Applicants are therefore requested to organise themselves accordingly taking into consideration possible waiting periods due to the online appointment
                            system.
The purpose of the proposed visit may not be altered once the visa has been issued. This is also applicable after entering the Schengen territory.
Above is regular information, if you wish to continue and to know more about the Visa application process, kindly click here (https://visa.vfsglobal.com/one-pager/germany/uae/english/).
Information on this website is subject to change, kindly check the information for updates before you apply.
Schengen visa must be obtained from the Embassy/Consulate of the country where the main purpose of travel lies. Therefore, please check your travel plans before submitting your application. In case of travel to multiple Schengen countries, please make your application to the one where you will be staying for the maximum number of days. If the duration of stay is the same in more than one Schengen country during the first trip, you must submit your visa application at the Embassy/Consulate of your first point of entry.
Please book appointments well in advance depending on your intended date of travel to avoid any inconvenience. Applications cannot be submitted earlier than six (6) months before the travel date. Please refer to the processing time specific to this Mission.

visa_types.overview:
Overview
For Persons applying for Airport Transit Visa
Entry clearance requirements depend on the citizenship and resident  status of the applicant (sometimes in a third country). Only a flight through one Schengen Union Country at a time is considered as a transit. The vast majority of travelers do not need a visa when air transiting through Germany. In Germany, the following airports
                                  have an International Transit Area: Cologne/Bonn (transit only possible between 4:30 am and 11:00 pm), Düsseldorf (transit only possible between
                                  6:00 am and 9:00 pm and only if the airline has arranged the transit with airport security – please check with your airline), Frankfurt/Main,  Hamburg (limited time
                                  frame only 4:30 am to 11:30 pm), Munich (München).
Please check with your travel agent / airline, if your flight is going via another airport. If you leave the airport’s international transit area, i.e. change the terminal, you will have to apply for a Schengen Visa – Category C.
PLEASE NOTE:
- Effective 11 June 2017, citizens of Ukraine can travel to Schengen countries without a prior visa for a period of up to 90 days. Please note that this exemption is only for holders
                                    of a Ukraine biometric passport. This does not apply to Ukrainian Nationals who are not holders of biometric passports and travelling for work during the stay period of 90 days.
- Effective 28 March 2017, citizens of Georgia can travel to Schengen countries without a prior visa for a period of up to 90 days. Please note that this exemption is only for holders
                                    of a Georgian biometric passport.
- Effective 15 March 2016, Peruvian nationals do not require a Schengen visa to travel to Germany for a period of 90 days or less within a timeframe of 190 days.

visa_types.overview:
Overview
Information about Long Term Visa
On this website, you find all relevant information to obtain a national visa for Germany at the Consulate General in Dubai. Please read this page carefully and follow all the instructions given!
Please note that VFS is only the external service provider authorized to accept appointment requests and National visa applications on behalf of the German missions in UAE. All procedures at VFS are in accordance with the guidelines of the German Consulate Dubai. VFS has no influence on the visa process itself – therefore, VFS cannot accelerate procedures or influence decisions. 
When is a National Visa required?
A National visa is required if your intended duration of stay in Germany is more than 90 days.
If you are unsure, which visa you need, click here:
https://www.auswaertiges-amt.de/en/visa-service/visa-navigator/2489244 (https://www.auswaertiges-amt.de/en/visa-service/visa-navigator/2489244)
Who is eligible to apply for a German National Visa at the German Consulate General in Dubai?
All applicants residing within the jurisdiction of the German Consulate General in Dubai are required to submit their German National Visa applications depending on the relevant visa category at the VFS Visa Application Centre in either Dubai or Abu Dhabi.
Customers are required to submit sufficient proof of residence together with their applications.

## Visa Fees

[Table:  Airport Transit]

[Table:  Airport Transit]

Please note:
- Please be informed that the fees charged by the Consulate General or VFS Global are based on the current exchange rate in local currency and may be subject to change without prior notification.
- Applicant shall be levied a service charge of AED 148.05 per application for Dubai and Abu Dhabi over and above visa fees. (all applicable taxes included)
- All fees are non-refundable.
Documents Required
Travel insurance is mandatory for all Schengen countries. If you have not purchased, please click here (https://visa.vfsglobal.com/are/en/deu/travel-insurance)
Kindly refer to the list of requisite documents below before submitting your application; this will minimize chances of rejection of your Visa Application due to incorrect or inadequate documents
The original passport must be submitted along with the application on the day of the appointment. Cabin crew/Pilots will be allowed to withdraw their passports from the respective application centre in Dubai or Abu Dhabi within 1 to 3 working days after their application is submitted at the Consulate.
Passports MUST be resubmitted at the Visa Application Centre within 10 to 12 calendar days from the date at which it was withdrawn. In case an applicant is aware in advance that this will not be possible due to professional reasons, the VFS counter staff must be informed to make a note on their application/checklist.
Instructions for applicants below 18 years
- For all children below 18 years, both parents need to be present at the Visa Application Centre to sign the VIDEX form. If you are applying for a visa for a child under 12 years old, they must also attend an appointment, but don’t have to provide their fingerprint information.
- In case one parent is out of the country during the application process, the signed authorization form and passport copy with exit stamps from UAE must be provided to complete the process.
- All applicants below 18 years must submit a signed letter of consent or authorization form and passport copy of the parent.
Click here to download the authorisation form sample.
Please ensure that you are well informed on the New Entry Regulations (https://uae.diplo.de/ae-en/service/05-VisaEinreise/-/2466976) for Germany.
Checklist
 Checklist for Documents for Medical Treatment
Insurance Declaration Form

Declaration of Consent

## Photo Specifications
Two recent biometric passport sized photographs (3.5x4.5 cm) as per specifications.
 Click here to view a sample biometric photo
Requirements:
- NEW! Should NOT be older than 6 months
- A frontal view of the face covering 67.20 -80% (32-26mm) of the photo
- A uniform and white-colored background
- Clear facial features and eyes – not covered by hair or glasses frame
- Head coverings are only accepted for religious reasons
- Do not glue or staple the pictures to the application form

## Processing Time
The processing time for the application at the German Consulate takes up to 15 calendar days.         
 Please be advised that due to the above mentioned minimum processing time, we are not able to guarantee the return of passports by specific dates for travel.                                   
Please be sure to submit your visa application well in advance, to avoid any inconveniences.

## Download Form
Below forms are required to be completed by each applicant to submit the visa application:
- Schengen Visa Application Form (https://videx.diplo.de/videx/visum-erfassung/videx-kurzfristiger-aufenthalt) (To be filled online - mandatory)
- Insurance Declaration Form (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/5BpmWlHkYKfUfWB3GuF3Du/7e038c5b6b95e25003740f7e2290d4e8/insurance-declaration-form.pdf)
- Declaration of Consent (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/2qM3R74t9pbZeNWq1cWMZO/1979b62ad5fa190c772d5480f7bfca59/declaration-of-consent.pdf)
All applicants below 18 years, must submit a signed letter of consent or authorization form and passport copy of the parents. Click here to download the authorisation form sample.
The Schengen Visa Application form must be filled either in English/German. 
Instructions to complete an online form: 
- Click on the form link above
- When the page opens complete the form
- For clarifications refer to the guidance (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/3jaqGLq16RSzLhEMevzkUE/5cbd5c49bdd79e0637dd1ae1337bae9e/VIDEX-General-User-Guidelines.pdf) instructions.
- Once complete print out the form on clear paper
- The complete the printed set of your application form including the last barcoded page of your application form should be presented while submitting your application.
- The barcode page contains the necessary data in condensed and machine readable format.

- The barcode page contains the necessary data in condensed and machine readable format.
- If you are not able to fill the form online, a form filling service is available at the Visa Application Centre.
